content_style: Clean page-wise study notes with main theory, definitions, key statutory requirements, standards, objectives, advantages and limitations.
chapter_outline: Statutory audit and the regulatory framework
Advantages and limitations of statutory audits

sections.title: Definition and objective of audit
paragraphs: An audit is an official examination of an entity's accounts or accounting system by an auditor. The auditor checks the financial statements and then gives an opinion on whether they have been prepared according to the applicable financial reporting framework.

Financial statements are a structured presentation of historical financial information. They communicate an entity's resources, obligations, performance, cash flows, equity changes, notes, and comparative information.

The applicable financial reporting framework is the framework selected by management and, where relevant, those charged with governance. It must be suitable for the entity and may be required by law, regulation, or accounting standards.

sections.title: Accountability, stewardship and agency
paragraphs: Audit is important because the owners of a company are often different from the people who manage it. Shareholders own the company, while directors manage and control it.
Directors have a stewardship role because they manage company assets on behalf of shareholders. This creates accountability, so directors must prepare annual financial statements for shareholders.
The agency concept applies because directors act as agents for shareholders, who are the principals. Agents must act in the principals' best interests and must be answerable for their actions.

pages.heading: Audit Report, Independence, Materiality and True and Fair View

sections.title: Purpose of the audit report
paragraphs: The annual audit developed as a way to add credibility to financial statements prepared by management. In a statutory audit, the auditor reports to shareholders on those financial statements.

The audit report is valuable because the auditor is independent from directors, gives an opinion on true and fair presentation, and considers whether the financial statements are fairly presented in all material respects.

sections.title: Independence
paragraphs: An external auditor must be independent from the directors. If the auditor is not independent, the opinion may be influenced by management and users may not rely on the report.
Internal auditors may have some independence, but they are usually not as independent as external auditors because they work within or for the organization.

sections.title: True and fair view
paragraphs: The auditor does not certify or guarantee that financial statements are completely correct. Instead, the auditor gives a professional judgement on whether the statements can be relied upon and are properly prepared.
True and fair view means the financial statements are not materially wrong or biased and present the entity's position and performance fairly under the relevant reporting framework.

sections.title: Materiality
paragraphs: Materiality means focusing on information that could influence users' economic decisions if it is omitted or misstated. The auditor gives an opinion on financial statements in all material respects.

pages.heading: Materiality and Statutory Requirement for Audit

sections.title: Applying materiality
paragraphs: Because of materiality, the auditor does not aim to check every number in the financial statements. The auditor focuses more effort on items with higher value or higher risk of misstatement.
A small error may be immaterial for a large entity, while a larger or riskier error may be material because it can affect users' decisions.

sections.title: Statutory audit requirement
paragraphs: Many countries require most companies to have an annual external audit by law. Smaller companies may be exempt, and non-company entities such as sole traders, partnerships, clubs, and societies may not always be legally required to have a statutory audit.
Some entities may still choose a voluntary audit even when the law does not require it, because an audit can increase credibility and confidence.

sections.title: First auditor
paragraphs: Under section 246 of the Companies Act 2017, the first auditor of a company is appointed by the board within 90 days from incorporation. The first auditor holds office until the conclusion of the first annual general meeting.

sections.title: Subsequent auditors
paragraphs: At every annual general meeting, the company appoints auditor or auditors on the board's recommendation after obtaining the auditors' consent. They hold office from the end of that meeting until the end of the next annual general meeting.

sections.title: Default in appointment
paragraphs: The Commission may step in where the company fails to appoint the first auditor within 90 days, fails to appoint auditors at the AGM, fails to fill a casual vacancy within 30 days, or where appointed auditors are unwilling to act.
If the company does not correct the default within the time specified by the Commission, the Commission may appoint auditors who hold office until the next annual general meeting.

sections.title: Casual vacancy and remuneration
paragraphs: A casual vacancy in the office of auditor is filled by the board within 30 days. If auditors are removed during their tenure, the board appoints new auditors with prior approval of the Commission.
Auditors' remuneration is fixed by the company in general meeting, or by the board or Commission where the auditor was appointed by them.

pages.heading: Auditor Resolutions, Removal, Qualification and Disqualification

sections.title: Resolutions for appointment
paragraphs: If a person other than the retiring auditor is proposed for appointment at an AGM, notice is required. Members holding at least 10 percent shareholding may propose an auditor after obtaining that auditor's consent.
The notice must be given to the company at least seven days before the AGM. The company sends the notice to the retiring auditor and posts it on its website.

The retiring auditor has a right to make a written representation at least two days before the general meeting. If representation is made, the auditor or authorized person must attend the meeting in person.

sections.title: Removal of auditors
paragraphs: Auditors appointed by the board or by members in an annual general meeting may be removed through a special resolution.

sections.title: Qualification of auditors
paragraphs: For a public company, a private company that is a subsidiary of a public company, or a private company with paid-up capital of three million rupees or more, the auditor must be a chartered accountant with a valid certificate of practice or a firm of chartered accountants.
For other companies, the auditor may be a chartered accountant or cost and management accountant with a valid certificate of practice, or an eligible firm, subject to the criteria specified.
Where a firm is appointed, it is appointed in the firm's name, but only partners who meet the qualification requirements can act and sign on behalf of the firm.

sections.title: Main disqualifications
paragraphs: A person cannot be auditor if independence is impaired, such as being a director, officer or employee of the company during the preceding three years, being connected with such persons, being spouse of a director, being indebted to the company beyond allowed ordinary-course limits, or having provided guarantee/security for another person's debt to the company.

Other disqualifications include business relationships outside the ordinary course of business, conviction for fraud within the specified period, being a body corporate, ineligibility under the relevant code of ethics, or holding shares in the audit client or associated companies subject to the stated disinvestment rule.

pages.heading: Effect of Disqualification and Auditor Rights

sections.title: Effect of disqualification
paragraphs: A person disqualified for appointment as auditor of a company is also not qualified for related companies such as the holding company, subsidiary, or fellow subsidiary where the law applies.

If an auditor becomes disqualified after appointment, the office is treated as vacated from the date of disqualification. Appointment of an unqualified or disqualified person is void, and the Commission may appoint a qualified person instead.
A person acting as auditor while not qualified may face a level 2 penalty on the standard scale.

sections.title: Rights of auditor
paragraphs: An auditor has access at all times to the company's books, accounts and vouchers in whatever form they are kept.

The auditor may access branch records transmitted to the principal office and may require information or explanations needed for audit duties from directors, officers, employees, persons responsible for records, subsidiaries, and relevant subsidiary personnel or auditors.
If a company officer unjustifiably blocks access, delays the auditor, fails to give required information or meeting notice, or provides false information, that officer may face a level 3 penalty.

pages.heading: Duties of Auditor and Audit Report Contents

sections.title: Audit work and report
paragraphs: The company's auditor conducts the audit and prepares the report in compliance with ISAs as adopted by ICAP.

The auditor performs examination sufficient to form an opinion on whether adequate accounting records have been kept, whether branch returns have been received where needed, and whether financial statements agree with accounting records and returns.

sections.title: Matters reported to members
paragraphs: The auditor reports to members on accounts, books of account, financial statements, and related documents laid before the company in general meeting.

The report states whether all necessary information and explanations were obtained, whether proper books were kept, whether statements comply with notified accounting and reporting standards, and whether the statements agree with books and returns.

The report also states whether the accounts provide required information and give a true and fair view of financial position, profit or loss and other comprehensive income or surplus/deficit, and cash flows.
The report includes whether investments, expenditure and guarantees were for company business, and whether zakat deductible at source was deducted and deposited as required.

sections.title: Negative or qualified answers
paragraphs: If any required matter is answered negatively or with qualification, the auditor's report must state the reason and the factual position to the best of the auditor's information.
The auditor expresses an unmodified or modified opinion according to ISAs adopted by ICAP.

pages.heading: Auditor Attendance, Signature, Cost Audit and Penalties

sections.title: Attendance at general meetings
paragraphs: The auditor is entitled to attend any general meeting, receive notices and communications that members receive, and be heard on business concerning the auditor. For a listed company, the auditor or authorized person must be present where financial statements and auditor's report are considered.

sections.title: Audit report signature
paragraphs: The auditor's report must state the name of the auditor and engagement partner, be signed and dated, and show the place of signing. An individual auditor signs personally, while a firm signs with the engagement partner's name.

sections.title: Audit of cost accounts
paragraphs: Where the Act requires certain cost records, the Commission may direct that the company's cost accounts be audited by a chartered accountant or cost and management accountant. That auditor has powers, duties and liabilities similar to a company auditor and any additional ones specified.

sections.title: Company and auditor default
paragraphs: If a company defaults in complying with specified audit-related sections, it may face a level 3 penalty, including a continuing daily penalty.

If an auditor's report, review report, or signed company document does not conform to the Act, is untrue, or fails to bring out material facts, the auditor and responsible signatories may face a level 2 penalty. If done with improper intent, imprisonment and a larger penalty may also apply.

sections.title: Role of auditing standards
paragraphs: Auditing standards help users trust that an audit has been carried out using established professional practice. They also support consistency when users compare companies audited by different auditors.
ISAs primarily apply to external audits, but their principles may also guide relevant internal audit work as good practice.

sections.title: IFEA and IAASB
paragraphs: The International Foundation for Ethics and Audit supports high-quality international standard setting in ethics, audit and assurance through the IAASB and the International Ethics Standards Board for Accountants.

The IAASB is an independent standard-setting board that serves the public interest by issuing high-quality auditing, assurance and related standards and encouraging convergence between international and national standards.

sections.title: Issuing ISAs and IAPSs
paragraphs: ICAP adopts ISAs issued by the IAASB, and this paper is based on knowledge of ISAs.
A new ISA is developed by selecting a subject, conducting study and research, issuing an exposure draft for comments, considering amendments, approving the final standard, and publishing it.
International Auditing Practice Statements do not have the same authority as ISAs. They help auditors implement ISAs and promote good auditing practice.

sections.title: Types of international standards
paragraphs: ISAs apply to audits of historical financial information. ISREs apply to reviews of historical financial information. ISAEs apply to assurance engagements other than audits or reviews of historical financial information. ISRSs apply to compilation, agreed-upon procedures and related services. ISQMs apply to all these services.

IAASB pronouncements do not override local law or regulation. If local requirements conflict with IAASB standards, a professional accountant should not claim compliance with IAASB standards unless all relevant standards have been fully complied with.

sections.title: Other standards and departures
paragraphs: Other international standards include basic principles and essential procedures, plus explanatory and other guidance. Essential procedures must be followed, unless exceptional circumstances justify departure to achieve the engagement objective.
Where departure from an essential procedure is considered necessary, the professional accountant must be ready to justify it.

sections.title: ISA 200 objectives
paragraphs: ISA 200 states that the auditor's objectives are to obtain reasonable assurance that financial statements as a whole are free from material misstatement, whether due to fraud or error, and to express an opinion on whether they are prepared in all material respects according to the applicable financial reporting framework.
The auditor also reports on the financial statements and communicates as required by ISAs according to the audit findings.

pages.heading: ISA 200 Requirements, Professional Skepticism, Judgement and Evidence

sections.title: Main ISA 200 requirements
paragraphs: ISA 200 requires the auditor to comply with all relevant ISAs, comply with ethical requirements, plan and perform the audit with professional skepticism, exercise professional judgement, and obtain sufficient appropriate audit evidence.

sections.title: Professional skepticism
paragraphs: Professional skepticism means having a questioning mind, staying alert to possible misstatement due to error or fraud, and critically assessing audit evidence.

Maintaining skepticism reduces the risk of overlooking unusual transactions, over-generalising from audit observations, and using inappropriate assumptions when planning, performing and evaluating audit procedures.

sections.title: Professional judgement
paragraphs: Professional judgement is the application of training, knowledge and experience within auditing, accounting and ethical standards to make informed decisions during the audit.
Judgement is used in materiality and audit risk, audit procedure design, evidence evaluation, evaluation of management judgement, and drawing conclusions from evidence obtained.

sections.title: Sufficient appropriate audit evidence
paragraphs: Sufficiency is the quantity of audit evidence, while appropriateness is the quality of evidence in terms of relevance and reliability. Quantity and quality are connected; more weak evidence cannot make up for poor quality.

pages.heading: Advantages and Limitations of Statutory Audits

sections.title: Advantages
paragraphs: External audit increases the credibility of published financial statements and confirms to management that statutory duties have been performed correctly.
Audit can provide assurance about compliance with non-statutory requirements such as corporate governance requirements where those are subject to audit or review.
Audit may give feedback on internal controls. If controls are weak or inadequate, the auditor may recommend improvements that help management reduce risk and improve company performance.
Even where statutory audit is not required, a voluntary audit can increase confidence for lenders and other users, and banks may require audited financial statements before lending.

sections.title: Limitations
paragraphs: Audit has limitations such as cost, disruption to company staff, uncertainty in estimates, concealed fraud, use of sampling, inherent limitations in client systems, and the fact that audit evidence is persuasive rather than conclusive.
Because of these limitations, an audit provides reasonable assurance, not an absolute guarantee that financial statements are correct in every respect or that all fraud will be detected.

ch1_p2_s1.english: An audit is an official examination of an entity's accounts or accounting system by an auditor.
ch1_p2_s1.urdu: Audit ka matlab hai kisi entity ke accounts ya accounting system ko official tareeqay se check karna. Auditor khud accounts nahi banata; woh check karta hai ke jo records banay hain woh reliable hain ya nahi.
ch1_p2_s1.example: Agar ABC Ltd ne sales, purchases aur bank records maintain kiye hain, auditor un records ko invoices, bank statements aur ledgers se match karke dekhega ke figures trust ke qabil hain ya nahi.

ch1_p2_s2.english: Financial statements are a structured presentation of historical financial information.
ch1_p2_s2.urdu: Financial statements past financial information ko proper format mein dikhati hain. In se user ko pata chalta hai company ke paas kya assets hain, kitna liability hai, profit/loss kya hai aur cash kahan se aya gaya.
ch1_p2_s2.example: Balance sheet batati hai company ke assets aur liabilities; profit and loss batata hai year mein profit hua ya loss; cash flow batata hai cash actually kahan se aya aur kahan use hua.

ch1_p2_s3.english: The applicable financial reporting framework is the framework selected by management and, where relevant, those charged with governance.
ch1_p2_s3.urdu: Applicable financial reporting framework woh rules ka set hai jiske mutabiq financial statements prepare hoti hain. Yeh management choose karta hai, lekin choice law, regulator aur accounting standards ke hisaab se acceptable honi chahiye.
ch1_p2_s3.example: Agar company Pakistan mein IFRS/IAS ke mutabiq accounts banane ki required hai, auditor check karega ke financial statements usi framework ke hisaab se prepared hain ya nahi.

ch1_p2_s4.english: Audit is important because the owners of a company are often different from the people who manage it.
ch1_p2_s4.urdu: Company mein paisa shareholders ka hota hai, lekin daily decisions directors lete hain. Jab owner aur manager alag hon, to independent audit zaroori hota hai taake owners ko sachchi picture mil sake.
ch1_p2_s4.example: Shareholders factory mein daily cash count nahi karte. Directors accounts prepare karte hain, aur auditor independently check karta hai ke directors ne shareholders ko fair information di hai ya nahi.

ch1_p2_s5.english: Directors have a stewardship role because they manage company assets on behalf of shareholders.
ch1_p2_s5.urdu: Stewardship ka simple matlab hai amanat sambhalna. Directors shareholders ke assets ko apni personal cheez nahi samajh sakte; unka kaam company ke resources ko shareholders ke benefit ke liye manage karna hai.
ch1_p2_s5.example: Agar company ke paas cash, inventory aur machinery hai, directors ki duty hai ke woh in assets ko protect karein aur business ke liye use karein, personal use ke liye nahi.

ch1_p2_s6.english: The agency concept applies because directors act as agents for shareholders, who are the principals.
ch1_p2_s6.urdu: Agency mein shareholders principal hote hain aur directors unke agents. Agent ko principal ke interest mein kaam karna hota hai aur baad mein report bhi deni hoti hai ke usne kya kiya.
ch1_p2_s6.example: Shareholders directors ko business chalane ka authority dete hain. Saal ke end par directors financial statements ke through batate hain ke company ki performance kaisi rahi.

ch1_p3_s1.english: The annual audit developed as a way to add credibility to financial statements prepared by management.
ch1_p3_s1.urdu: Management apne accounts khud banati hai, isliye users ko doubt ho sakta hai. Annual audit ek independent check add karta hai, jis se financial statements ki credibility barhti hai.
ch1_p3_s1.example: Bank loan dene se pehle audited financial statements maang sakta hai kyunke auditor ki report bank ko figures par zyada confidence deti hai.

ch1_p3_s2.english: The audit report is valuable because the auditor is independent from directors, gives an opinion on true and fair presentation, and considers whether the financial statements are fairly presented in all material respects.
ch1_p3_s2.urdu: Audit report ki value is wajah se hoti hai ke auditor independent hota hai, true and fair view par opinion deta hai, aur sirf important yani material matters ko focus karta hai.
ch1_p3_s2.example: Auditor yeh nahi likhta ke har choti entry 100% perfect hai; woh opinion deta hai ke financial statements overall important matters mein fair picture de rahi hain ya nahi.

ch1_p3_s3.english: An external auditor must be independent from the directors.
ch1_p3_s3.urdu: External auditor ko directors se independent hona chahiye. Agar auditor management ke pressure mein ho, to uski opinion users ke liye reliable nahi rahegi.
ch1_p3_s3.example: Agar auditor company ke finance director ka business partner bhi ho, to shareholders sochenge ke auditor sach mein neutral hai ya management ko protect kar raha hai.

ch1_p3_s4.english: The auditor does not certify or guarantee that financial statements are completely correct.
ch1_p3_s4.urdu: Auditor guarantee nahi deta ke statements bilkul perfect hain. Auditor reasonable audit work ke baad opinion deta hai; audit absolute certificate nahi hota.
ch1_p3_s4.example: Auditor sampling use karta hai, har single invoice check nahi karta. Isliye report assurance deti hai, full guarantee nahi ke koi bhi choti ghalti exist nahi karti.

ch1_p3_s5.english: True and fair view means the financial statements are not materially wrong or biased and present the entity's position and performance fairly under the relevant reporting framework.
ch1_p3_s5.urdu: True and fair view ka matlab hai financial statements important level par sachchi aur unbiased picture de rahi hain. Estimates aur judgement allowed hain, lekin woh reasonable honi chahiye.
ch1_p3_s5.example: Receivables mein bad debts ka estimate lagana judgement hai. Auditor dekhega ke management ne bad debt estimate fair basis par lagaya hai ya profit zyada dikhane ke liye kam rakha hai.

ch1_p3_s6.english: Materiality means focusing on information that could influence users' economic decisions if it is omitted or misstated.
ch1_p3_s6.urdu: Material information woh hoti hai jo user ke decision ko change kar sakti hai. Agar koi error ya omission investor, bank ya shareholder ke decision ko affect kare, to woh material hai.
ch1_p3_s6.example: Rs. 500 petty cash ki ghalti large company mein material nahi hogi, lekin Rs. 5 million receivable jo recover nahi ho sakta, investor ke decision ko affect kar sakta hai.

ch1_p4_s1.english: Because of materiality, the auditor does not aim to check every number in the financial statements.
ch1_p4_s1.urdu: Materiality ki wajah se auditor har number check nahi karta. Auditor apni energy un areas par lagata hai jahan amount bara ho ya misstatement ka chance zyada ho.
ch1_p4_s1.example: Auditor har stationery bill check karne ke bajaye inventory, revenue, receivables aur bank balances jese important areas par zyada audit procedures karega.

ch1_p4_s2.english: A small error may be immaterial for a large entity, while a larger or riskier error may be material because it can affect users' decisions.
ch1_p4_s2.urdu: Choti ghalti kabhi immaterial hoti hai, lekin bari amount ya risky item material ho sakta hai. Materiality company ke size aur situation ke hisaab se judge hoti hai.
ch1_p4_s2.example: Rs. 100,000 ki error multinational company mein choti ho sakti hai, lekin ek small company ke liye yehi amount profit ko loss mein badal sakti hai.

ch1_p4_s3.english: Many countries require most companies to have an annual external audit by law.
ch1_p4_s3.urdu: Statutory audit woh audit hai jo law require karta hai. Bohat si companies ko har saal external auditor se financial statements audit karwana parta hai.
ch1_p4_s3.example: Companies Act ke under eligible companies ko annual financial statements par auditor ki report leni hoti hai aur shareholders ke saamne present karni hoti hai.

ch1_p4_s4.english: Some entities may still choose a voluntary audit even when the law does not require it, because an audit can increase credibility and confidence.
ch1_p4_s4.urdu: Voluntary audit law ki compulsion ke baghair hota hai. Entity khud audit karwati hai taake accounts par trust barhe ya lender/investor satisfy ho jaye.
ch1_p4_s4.example: Ek partnership business bank loan lena chahta hai. Law audit require nahi karta, lekin bank ke confidence ke liye partners voluntary audit karwa lete hain.

ch1_p5_s1.english: Under section 246 of the Companies Act 2017, the first auditor of a company is appointed by the board within 90 days from incorporation.
ch1_p5_s1.urdu: Company banne ke baad pehla auditor board appoint karta hai, aur yeh appointment incorporation ke 90 din ke andar honi chahiye. First auditor first AGM tak office hold karta hai.
ch1_p5_s1.example: Agar company 1 January ko incorporate hui, to board ko 31 March tak first auditor appoint karna hoga. Woh auditor first AGM ke end tak rahega.

ch1_p5_s2.english: At every annual general meeting, the company appoints auditor or auditors on the board's recommendation after obtaining the auditors' consent.
ch1_p5_s2.urdu: Baad ke auditors har AGM mein appoint hote hain. Board recommendation deta hai, auditor se consent li jati hai, phir auditor next AGM tak office hold karta hai.
ch1_p5_s2.example: 2026 ki AGM mein M/s Khan & Co appoint hue, to woh 2027 ki AGM ke conclusion tak company ke auditors rahenge.

ch1_p5_s3.english: The Commission may step in where the company fails to appoint the first auditor within 90 days, fails to appoint auditors at the AGM, fails to fill a casual vacancy within 30 days, or where appointed auditors are unwilling to act.
ch1_p5_s3.urdu: Agar company auditor appoint karne mein default kare, to Commission intervene kar sakti hai. Law company ko auditor ke baghair chhorna nahi chahta.
ch1_p5_s3.example: Company ne first auditor 90 din mein appoint nahi kiya. Member SECP ko application de sakta hai, aur Commission company ko default correct karne ka order de sakti hai.

ch1_p5_s4.english: If the company does not correct the default within the time specified by the Commission, the Commission may appoint auditors who hold office until the next annual general meeting.
ch1_p5_s4.urdu: Agar company Commission ke kehne ke bawajood default fix na kare, to Commission khud auditor appoint kar sakti hai. Yeh auditor next AGM tak office hold karega.
ch1_p5_s4.example: SECP ne company ko 15 din diye auditor appoint karne ke liye, lekin company ne nahi kiya. Ab SECP qualified auditor appoint kar sakti hai.

ch1_p5_s5.english: A casual vacancy in the office of auditor is filled by the board within 30 days.
ch1_p5_s5.urdu: Casual vacancy ka matlab auditor ki seat beech saal mein khali ho jana, jese resignation ya death. Board ko 30 din ke andar naya auditor appoint karna hota hai.
ch1_p5_s5.example: Auditor March mein resign kar gaya, jabke AGM December mein hai. Board ko April ke andar casual vacancy fill karni hogi.

ch1_p5_s6.english: Auditors' remuneration is fixed by the company in general meeting, or by the board or Commission where the auditor was appointed by them.
ch1_p5_s6.urdu: Auditor ki fee ya remuneration woh authority fix karti hai jis ne auditor appoint kiya. General meeting appoint kare to company fee fix karegi; board/Commission appoint kare to woh fee fix karenge.
ch1_p5_s6.example: Agar AGM mein auditor appoint hua, shareholders fee approve kar sakte hain. Agar SECP ne auditor appoint kiya, to SECP remuneration decide kar sakti hai.

ch1_p6_s1.english: If a person other than the retiring auditor is proposed for appointment at an AGM, notice is required.
ch1_p6_s1.urdu: Agar retiring auditor ke bajaye koi naya auditor appoint karna ho, to members ko proper notice dena zaroori hai. Is se retiring auditor aur members dono ko pehle se pata chal jata hai.
ch1_p6_s1.example: Company ke current auditor A & Co hain. Members B & Co ko appoint karna chahte hain, to AGM se pehle proper notice company ko dena hoga.

ch1_p6_s2.english: The notice must be given to the company at least seven days before the AGM.
ch1_p6_s2.urdu: Notice AGM se kam az kam 7 din pehle company ko dena hota hai. Is timing ka purpose yeh hai ke company retiring auditor ko inform kar sake aur members ko proposal ka pata chal sake.
ch1_p6_s2.example: AGM 20 June ko hai. Naye auditor ka notice 13 June ya us se pehle company ko mil jana chahiye.

ch1_p6_s3.english: The retiring auditor has a right to make a written representation at least two days before the general meeting.
ch1_p6_s3.urdu: Retiring auditor ko apni side explain karne ka right hota hai. Agar usay replace kiya ja raha ho, woh written representation de sakta hai jo meeting mein consider hoti hai.
ch1_p6_s3.example: Current auditor ko lagta hai usay unfairly replace kiya ja raha hai. Woh AGM se 2 din pehle written representation dekar shareholders ko apni baat bata sakta hai.

ch1_p6_s4.english: Auditors appointed by the board or by members in an annual general meeting may be removed through a special resolution.
ch1_p6_s4.urdu: Auditor ko remove karna serious matter hai, isliye normal board decision enough nahi. Special resolution chahiye taake shareholders formally approve karein.
ch1_p6_s4.example: Board ne kaha auditor ko hata do, lekin law ke mutabiq shareholders ki special resolution ke baghair removal valid nahi hogi.

ch1_p6_s5.english: For a public company, a private company that is a subsidiary of a public company, or a private company with paid-up capital of three million rupees or more, the auditor must be a chartered accountant with a valid certificate of practice or a firm of chartered accountants.
ch1_p6_s5.urdu: Public companies aur kuch bari private companies ke liye auditor qualified chartered accountant ya CA firm hona chahiye. Is ka reason yeh hai ke public interest aur complexity zyada hoti hai.
ch1_p6_s5.example: Listed company ka audit koi unqualified accountant nahi kar sakta; us ke liye practicing CA ya eligible CA firm required hogi.

ch1_p6_s6.english: For other companies, the auditor may be a chartered accountant or cost and management accountant with a valid certificate of practice, or an eligible firm, subject to the criteria specified.
ch1_p6_s6.urdu: Other companies ke liye law CA ya CMA ko allow kar sakta hai, lekin certificate of practice valid hona zaroori hai. Sirf degree hona enough nahi hota.
ch1_p6_s6.example: Ek small private company eligible practicing CMA firm ko auditor appoint kar sakti hai, agar law ke criteria meet ho rahe hon.

ch1_p6_s7.english: Where a firm is appointed, it is appointed in the firm's name, but only partners who meet the qualification requirements can act and sign on behalf of the firm.
ch1_p6_s7.urdu: Firm appoint hoti hai firm ke naam se, lekin kaam aur signing sirf qualified partners kar sakte hain. Har partner automatically audit sign karne ka authorized nahi hota.
ch1_p6_s7.example: ABC Chartered Accountants firm appoint hui. Report woh partner sign karega jiske paas valid practice qualification hai, trainee ya non-qualified partner nahi.

ch1_p6_s8.english: A person cannot be auditor if independence is impaired, such as being a director, officer or employee of the company during the preceding three years, being connected with such persons, being spouse of a director, being indebted to the company beyond allowed ordinary-course limits, or having provided guarantee/security for another person's debt to the company.
ch1_p6_s8.urdu: Disqualification rules auditor ki independence protect karte hain. Agar auditor company se job, family, loan ya guarantee ke through linked ho, to uska opinion biased ho sakta hai.
ch1_p6_s8.example: Agar auditor company ka ex-finance manager tha aur pichle 3 saal mein employee raha, to usay auditor appoint karna independence issue create karega.

ch1_p6_s9.english: Other disqualifications include business relationships outside the ordinary course of business, conviction for fraud within the specified period, being a body corporate, ineligibility under the relevant code of ethics, or holding shares in the audit client or associated companies subject to the stated disinvestment rule.
ch1_p6_s9.urdu: Shares rakhna ya unusual business relationship rakhna bhi independence ko damage kar sakta hai. Auditor ko client se aisa financial interest nahi hona chahiye jis se opinion affect ho.
ch1_p6_s9.example: Agar audit partner ke minor child ke paas audit client ke shares hain, to auditor ko disclose/disinvest rules follow karne honge warna appointment problem mein aa sakti hai.

ch1_p7_s1.english: A person disqualified for appointment as auditor of a company is also not qualified for related companies such as the holding company, subsidiary, or fellow subsidiary where the law applies.
ch1_p7_s1.urdu: Disqualification sirf ek company tak limited nahi reh sakti. Agar relationship holding/subsidiary group se linked ho, to auditor related companies ke liye bhi disqualified ho sakta hai.
ch1_p7_s1.example: Auditor parent company ka employee raha hai. Agar subsidiary ka audit lena ho to us relationship ki wajah se subsidiary audit mein bhi disqualification issue aa sakta hai.

ch1_p7_s2.english: If an auditor becomes disqualified after appointment, the office is treated as vacated from the date of disqualification.
ch1_p7_s2.urdu: Agar appointment ke baad auditor disqualified ho jaye, to uski seat usi date se vacant samjhi jati hai. Company ko phir replacement ka process follow karna hota hai.
ch1_p7_s2.example: Audit ke beech auditor ne client company ke shares kharid liye. Disqualification date se uska office vacant maana ja sakta hai.

ch1_p7_s3.english: A person acting as auditor while not qualified may face a level 2 penalty on the standard scale.
ch1_p7_s3.urdu: Agar koi person qualified nahi aur phir bhi auditor ban kar act kare, to us par penalty lag sakti hai. Law audit ko qualified professional se hi karwana chahta hai.
ch1_p7_s3.example: Ek accountant jiske paas valid certificate of practice nahi, public company ka audit sign karta hai. Us par level 2 penalty lag sakti hai.

ch1_p7_s4.english: An auditor has access at all times to the company's books, accounts and vouchers in whatever form they are kept.
ch1_p7_s4.urdu: Auditor ko company ke books, accounts aur vouchers tak access ka legal right hota hai. Records paper mein hon ya electronic system mein, auditor unhein dekh sakta hai.
ch1_p7_s4.example: Auditor sales invoices, purchase bills, bank statements, ledger reports aur ERP system records inspect kar sakta hai.

ch1_p7_s5.english: The auditor may access branch records transmitted to the principal office and may require information or explanations needed for audit duties from directors, officers, employees, persons responsible for records, subsidiaries, and relevant subsidiary personnel or auditors.
ch1_p7_s5.urdu: Auditor sirf documents nahi dekhta; zaroorat par directors, employees, branch staff ya subsidiary se explanations bhi maang sakta hai. Explanation audit evidence ko samajhne mein help karti hai.
ch1_p7_s5.example: Inventory difference aya to auditor warehouse manager se pooch sakta hai ke shortage damage ki wajah se hai, theft ki wajah se, ya counting error ki wajah se.

ch1_p7_s6.english: If a company officer unjustifiably blocks access, delays the auditor, fails to give required information or meeting notice, or provides false information, that officer may face a level 3 penalty.
ch1_p7_s6.urdu: Agar officer auditor ko records se roke, delay kare, false information de, ya meeting notice na de, to yeh serious default hai. Law auditor ke work ko obstruct karne par penalty lagata hai.
ch1_p7_s6.example: Finance manager auditor ko bank reconciliation dene se mana kar deta hai ya fake ledger provide karta hai. Is par officer penalty face kar sakta hai.

ch1_p8_s1.english: The company's auditor conducts the audit and prepares the report in compliance with ISAs as adopted by ICAP.
ch1_p8_s1.urdu: Company audit personal style se nahi hota; auditor ko ICAP adopted ISAs follow karne hote hain. ISAs batate hain audit plan, evidence, documentation aur reporting ka professional method.
ch1_p8_s1.example: Auditor revenue check karte waqt ISA ke mutabiq risk assess karega, procedures design karega, evidence collect karega aur working papers maintain karega.

ch1_p8_s2.english: The auditor performs examination sufficient to form an opinion on whether adequate accounting records have been kept, whether branch returns have been received where needed, and whether financial statements agree with accounting records and returns.
ch1_p8_s2.urdu: Auditor check karta hai ke accounting records adequate hain aur financial statements un records se agree karti hain. Agar records weak hon to opinion form karna mushkil ho sakta hai.
ch1_p8_s2.example: Trial balance mein receivables Rs. 10 million hain, lekin ledger Rs. 8 million show karta hai. Auditor is mismatch ko investigate karega.

ch1_p8_s3.english: The auditor reports to members on accounts, books of account, financial statements, and related documents laid before the company in general meeting.
ch1_p8_s3.urdu: Auditor ki report members/shareholders ke liye hoti hai. Is report mein auditor accounts, books aur financial statements par apni opinion communicate karta hai.
ch1_p8_s3.example: AGM mein shareholders financial statements ke sath auditor report bhi dekhte hain taake unhein pata chale accounts par independent opinion kya hai.

ch1_p8_s4.english: The report states whether all necessary information and explanations were obtained, whether proper books were kept, whether statements comply with notified accounting and reporting standards, and whether the statements agree with books and returns.
ch1_p8_s4.urdu: Auditor report mein batata hai ke usay required information aur explanations mil gayi hain ya nahi. Agar information nahi mili to users ko pata chalna chahiye ke audit limitation thi.
ch1_p8_s4.example: Management ne auditor ko major supplier confirmations provide nahi ki. Auditor report mein bata sakta hai ke necessary evidence obtain nahi hua.

ch1_p8_s5.english: The report also states whether the accounts provide required information and give a true and fair view of financial position, profit or loss and other comprehensive income or surplus/deficit, and cash flows.
ch1_p8_s5.urdu: Auditor yeh opinion deta hai ke accounts law ke required information de rahe hain aur company ki financial position, performance aur cash flows ka true and fair view show kar rahe hain ya nahi.
ch1_p8_s5.example: Agar company obsolete inventory ko full cost par show kar rahi hai, financial position overstated ho sakti hai aur auditor clean opinion dene se pehle adjustment maangega.

ch1_p8_s6.english: The report includes whether investments, expenditure and guarantees were for company business, and whether zakat deductible at source was deducted and deposited as required.
ch1_p8_s6.urdu: Auditor kuch specific legal matters bhi report karta hai, jese investments/expenditure business purpose ke liye thay ya nahi, aur zakat deductible thi to deduct/deposit hui ya nahi.
ch1_p8_s6.example: Company ne director ke personal house renovation par company expense book kar diya. Auditor question karega ke yeh company business purpose ke liye tha ya nahi.

ch1_p8_s7.english: If any required matter is answered negatively or with qualification, the auditor's report must state the reason and the factual position to the best of the auditor's information.
ch1_p8_s7.urdu: Agar auditor kisi required matter par negative answer de ya qualification lagaye, to sirf 'no' likhna enough nahi. Reason aur factual position explain karni hoti hai.
ch1_p8_s7.example: Auditor ko inventory count attend nahi karne diya gaya. Report mein auditor reason batayega aur explain karega ke inventory evidence obtain nahi ho saka.

ch1_p8_s8.english: The auditor expresses an unmodified or modified opinion according to ISAs adopted by ICAP.
ch1_p8_s8.urdu: Unmodified opinion ka matlab clean opinion; modified opinion ka matlab koi issue hai jise report mein highlight kiya gaya. Auditor opinion ISA rules ke mutabiq choose karta hai.
ch1_p8_s8.example: Agar financial statements overall fair hain to unmodified opinion. Agar material inventory error adjust nahi hua to qualified/adverse opinion ho sakti hai.

ch1_p9_s1.english: The auditor is entitled to attend any general meeting, receive notices and communications that members receive, and be heard on business concerning the auditor.
ch1_p9_s1.urdu: Auditor ko general meeting attend karne aur audit se related matters par bolne ka right hota hai. Shareholders agar audit report par sawal karein to auditor clarify kar sakta hai.
ch1_p9_s1.example: AGM mein shareholder poochta hai ke auditor ne receivables par qualification kyun di. Auditor meeting mein apni audit concern explain kar sakta hai.

ch1_p9_s2.english: The auditor's report must state the name of the auditor and engagement partner, be signed and dated, and show the place of signing.
ch1_p9_s2.urdu: Audit report formal document hai, isliye auditor ka naam, engagement partner, signature, date aur signing place mention hona zaroori hai. Yeh accountability create karta hai.
ch1_p9_s2.example: Report ke end par 'Khan & Co, Chartered Accountants', engagement partner ka naam, Lahore, aur signing date likhi hogi.

ch1_p9_s3.english: Where the Act requires certain cost records, the Commission may direct that the company's cost accounts be audited by a chartered accountant or cost and management accountant.
ch1_p9_s3.urdu: Kuch companies ko cost records maintain karne hote hain. Commission direct kare to in cost accounts ka separate audit CA ya CMA se karwaya ja sakta hai.
ch1_p9_s3.example: Manufacturing sector mein regulator cost of production records ka audit require kar sakta hai taake product costing reliable ho.

ch1_p9_s4.english: If a company defaults in complying with specified audit-related sections, it may face a level 3 penalty, including a continuing daily penalty.
ch1_p9_s4.urdu: Agar company audit related legal sections comply na kare, to company par heavy penalty lag sakti hai. Continuing default mein daily penalty bhi add hoti rehti hai.
ch1_p9_s4.example: Company auditor appoint nahi karti aur default continue hota rehta hai. Law ke under company ko level 3 penalty aur daily penalty face karni par sakti hai.

ch1_p9_s5.english: If an auditor's report, review report, or signed company document does not conform to the Act, is untrue, or fails to bring out material facts, the auditor and responsible signatories may face a level 2 penalty.
ch1_p9_s5.urdu: Agar auditor ki report law ke mutabiq nahi, untrue hai, ya material facts hide karti hai, to auditor responsible ho sakta hai. Serious dishonest intent ho to imprisonment ka risk bhi hota hai.
ch1_p9_s5.example: Auditor ko pata hai company ne major fraud hide kiya hai, phir bhi clean report sign kar deta hai. Yeh auditor default aur serious legal consequence create kar sakta hai.

ch1_p19_s1.english: Auditing standards help users trust that an audit has been carried out using established professional practice.
ch1_p19_s1.urdu: Auditing standards audit ko ek professional framework dete hain. Users ko confidence hota hai ke auditor ne apni marzi se nahi, accepted standards ke mutabiq kaam kiya.
ch1_p19_s1.example: Bank ko audited statements is liye zyada reliable lagti hain kyunke auditor ne ISA based procedures perform kiye hote hain, sirf casual checking nahi.

ch1_p19_s2.english: ISAs primarily apply to external audits, but their principles may also guide relevant internal audit work as good practice.
ch1_p19_s2.urdu: ISAs mainly external audit ke liye hotay hain, lekin unke principles internal audit mein bhi good practice ke tor par useful ho sakte hain.
ch1_p19_s2.example: Internal auditor purchase controls review karte waqt ISA style risk assessment aur evidence documentation approach use kar sakta hai.

ch1_p19_s3.english: The International Foundation for Ethics and Audit supports high-quality international standard setting in ethics, audit and assurance through the IAASB and the International Ethics Standards Board for Accountants.
ch1_p19_s3.urdu: IFEA international level par ethics, audit aur assurance standards ki quality support karta hai. Iska purpose public interest aur profession par trust improve karna hai.
ch1_p19_s3.example: Jab world level par common high-quality standards bante hain, to different countries ke auditors ka kaam users ko zyada consistent aur trustworthy lagta hai.

ch1_p19_s4.english: The IAASB is an independent standard-setting board that serves the public interest by issuing high-quality auditing, assurance and related standards and encouraging convergence between international and national standards.
ch1_p19_s4.urdu: IAASB independent board hai jo auditing aur assurance standards issue karta hai. Yeh standards profession ko guide karte hain aur public confidence build karte hain.
ch1_p19_s4.example: ISA 200 jese standards IAASB issue karta hai, jisse auditor ko pata chalta hai audit ke overall objectives aur conduct kya hone chahiye.

ch1_p19_s5.english: ICAP adopts ISAs issued by the IAASB, and this paper is based on knowledge of ISAs.
ch1_p19_s5.urdu: Pakistan mein ICAP IAASB ke issued ISAs adopt karta hai. Is paper mein bhi audit concepts ISAs ke knowledge par based hain.
ch1_p19_s5.example: CAF audit student ISA 200, ISA 315 ya ISA 500 parhta hai kyunke Pakistan mein audit practice ICAP adopted ISAs se linked hai.

ch1_p19_s6.english: A new ISA is developed by selecting a subject, conducting study and research, issuing an exposure draft for comments, considering amendments, approving the final standard, and publishing it.
ch1_p19_s6.urdu: ISA banane ka process proper due process follow karta hai. Pehle topic study hota hai, phir exposure draft issue hota hai, comments liye jate hain, phir final standard approve hota hai.
ch1_p19_s6.example: Agar audit evidence par new guidance chahiye ho, IAASB pehle draft public comment ke liye release karega, profession feedback dega, phir final ISA publish hoga.

ch1_p19_s7.english: International Auditing Practice Statements do not have the same authority as ISAs.
ch1_p19_s7.urdu: IAPSs guidance documents hotay hain. Yeh auditors ko ISAs apply karne mein help karte hain, lekin inki authority ISA jaisi strong nahi hoti.
ch1_p19_s7.example: Auditor ISA ki requirement follow karega. IAPS usay practical guidance de sakta hai ke kisi industry situation mein ISA ko apply kaise karna hai.

ch1_p20_s1.english: ISAs apply to audits of historical financial information.
ch1_p20_s1.urdu: ISAs historical financial information ke audit par apply hotay hain. Yani jab auditor past financial statements ka audit kar raha ho, to ISAs relevant hotay hain.
ch1_p20_s1.example: Company ke 31 December 2026 financial statements ka statutory audit ISAs ke under perform kiya jayega.

ch1_p20_s2.english: IAASB pronouncements do not override local law or regulation.
ch1_p20_s2.urdu: IAASB standards local law ko override nahi karte. Agar local law koi specific requirement rakhta hai, auditor ko law aur relevant standards dono ko carefully consider karna hota hai.
ch1_p20_s2.example: Agar Companies Act report mein specific wording require karta hai, auditor sirf international template use karke local legal wording ignore nahi kar sakta.

ch1_p20_s3.english: Other international standards include basic principles and essential procedures, plus explanatory and other guidance.
ch1_p20_s3.urdu: Standards mein essential procedures woh steps hain jo normally follow karna zaroori hota hai. Guidance auditor ko samjhati hai ke requirement apply kaise karni hai.
ch1_p20_s3.example: Agar standard kehta hai auditor must obtain sufficient appropriate evidence, to auditor evidence ke baghair sirf management ki baat par opinion nahi de sakta.

ch1_p20_s4.english: Where departure from an essential procedure is considered necessary, the professional accountant must be ready to justify it.
ch1_p20_s4.urdu: Agar exceptional situation mein auditor essential procedure follow nahi karta, to uske paas strong reason aur documentation honi chahiye. Departure casual choice nahi hoti.
ch1_p20_s4.example: Agar physical inventory count attend nahi ho saka, auditor ko alternative procedures perform karke justify karna hoga ke evidence kaise obtain hua.

ch1_p20_s5.english: ISA 200 states that the auditor's objectives are to obtain reasonable assurance that financial statements as a whole are free from material misstatement, whether due to fraud or error, and to express an opinion on whether they are prepared in all material respects according to the applicable financial reporting framework.
ch1_p20_s5.urdu: ISA 200 ka core objective hai reasonable assurance lena ke financial statements as a whole material misstatement se free hain, chahe misstatement fraud se ho ya error se.
ch1_p20_s5.example: Auditor revenue, expenses, assets aur liabilities par procedures karta hai taake opinion de sake ke statements overall materially wrong nahi hain.

ch1_p20_s6.english: The auditor also reports on the financial statements and communicates as required by ISAs according to the audit findings.
ch1_p20_s6.urdu: Audit ke end par auditor findings ke basis par report karta hai aur ISAs ke required communications karta hai. Sirf testing karna enough nahi, result communicate bhi karna hota hai.
ch1_p20_s6.example: Agar auditor ko internal control weakness milti hai, woh management/those charged with governance ko communicate kar sakta hai aur report mein opinion bhi issue karta hai.

ch1_p21_s1.english: ISA 200 requires the auditor to comply with all relevant ISAs, comply with ethical requirements, plan and perform the audit with professional skepticism, exercise professional judgement, and obtain sufficient appropriate audit evidence.
ch1_p21_s1.urdu: ISA 200 auditor se demand karta hai ke relevant ISAs follow kare, ethics comply kare, skepticism aur judgement use kare, aur sufficient appropriate audit evidence obtain kare.
ch1_p21_s1.example: Auditor client ki baat blindly accept nahi karega; woh ethical independence maintain karega, risks assess karega aur evidence collect karke opinion banayega.

ch1_p21_s2.english: Professional skepticism means having a questioning mind, staying alert to possible misstatement due to error or fraud, and critically assessing audit evidence.
ch1_p21_s2.urdu: Professional skepticism ka matlab healthy doubt rakhna. Auditor har baat par shaq nahi karta, lekin evidence ko critically evaluate karta hai aur fraud/error ke indicators par alert rehta hai.
ch1_p21_s2.example: Client kehta hai receivables 100% recoverable hain, lekin customers old outstanding hain. Skeptical auditor confirmations aur subsequent receipts check karega.

ch1_p21_s3.english: Maintaining skepticism reduces the risk of overlooking unusual transactions, over-generalising from audit observations, and using inappropriate assumptions when planning, performing and evaluating audit procedures.
ch1_p21_s3.urdu: Skepticism auditor ko unusual transactions miss karne, limited evidence se jaldi conclusion nikalne, aur weak assumptions use karne se bachata hai.
ch1_p21_s3.example: Year-end par unusually large sales book hui hain. Skeptical auditor delivery documents aur customer confirmations check karega before accepting revenue.

ch1_p21_s4.english: Professional judgement is the application of training, knowledge and experience within auditing, accounting and ethical standards to make informed decisions during the audit.
ch1_p21_s4.urdu: Professional judgement ka matlab auditor apni training, knowledge aur experience use karke informed decision leta hai. Har audit situation ka answer formula se nahi nikalta.
ch1_p21_s4.example: Auditor decide karta hai inventory risk high hai, isliye stock count observe karna aur more sample testing karna necessary hai. Yeh professional judgement hai.

ch1_p21_s5.english: Judgement is used in materiality and audit risk, audit procedure design, evidence evaluation, evaluation of management judgement, and drawing conclusions from evidence obtained.
ch1_p21_s5.urdu: Audit mein judgement materiality set karne, risk assess karne, procedures choose karne, evidence evaluate karne aur final conclusion nikalne mein use hoti hai.
ch1_p21_s5.example: Auditor decide karta hai Rs. 2 million error material hai ya nahi, aur kya additional testing chahiye. Yeh judgement without proper experience mushkil hoti hai.

ch1_p21_s6.english: Sufficiency is the quantity of audit evidence, while appropriateness is the quality of evidence in terms of relevance and reliability.
ch1_p21_s6.urdu: Sufficiency evidence ki quantity hai, appropriateness evidence ki quality. Dono zaroori hain; bohat zyada weak evidence ek strong reliable evidence ka substitute nahi ban sakti.
ch1_p21_s6.example: Client ke 20 internal emails se behtar bank se direct confirmation ho sakti hai, kyunke external evidence zyada reliable hota hai.

ch1_p23_s1.english: External audit increases the credibility of published financial statements and confirms to management that statutory duties have been performed correctly.
ch1_p23_s1.urdu: External audit published financial statements ki credibility barhata hai. Users ko lagta hai ke figures par independent professional ne check kiya hai.
ch1_p23_s1.example: Investor unaudited accounts se hesitate kar sakta hai, lekin audited financial statements aur clean audit report dekh kar zyada confidence feel karta hai.

ch1_p23_s2.english: Audit can provide assurance about compliance with non-statutory requirements such as corporate governance requirements where those are subject to audit or review.
ch1_p23_s2.urdu: Audit management ko yeh comfort de sakta hai ke company ne kuch non-statutory requirements, jese governance requirements, properly follow ki hain jahan review/audit required ho.
ch1_p23_s2.example: Listed company corporate governance statement prepare karti hai. Auditor/reviewer check kar sakta hai ke required governance disclosures properly included hain.

ch1_p23_s3.english: Audit may give feedback on internal controls.
ch1_p23_s3.urdu: Audit ke during auditor internal controls ki weakness notice kar sakta hai. Weak controls par recommendations management ko risk reduce karne mein help karti hain.
ch1_p23_s3.example: Auditor dekhta hai cash receipts par segregation of duties nahi. Woh recommend karega ke cash receive aur record karne wale persons separate hon.

ch1_p23_s4.english: Even where statutory audit is not required, a voluntary audit can increase confidence for lenders and other users, and banks may require audited financial statements before lending.
ch1_p23_s4.urdu: Jahan statutory audit required nahi hota, wahan bhi voluntary audit lenders aur users ka trust barha sakta hai. Banks aksar loan se pehle audited accounts prefer karte hain.
ch1_p23_s4.example: Small private company legally exempt hai, lekin bank loan ke liye audited accounts submit karti hai taake bank ko figures reliable lagen.

ch1_p23_s5.english: Audit has limitations such as cost, disruption to company staff, uncertainty in estimates, concealed fraud, use of sampling, inherent limitations in client systems, and the fact that audit evidence is persuasive rather than conclusive.
ch1_p23_s5.urdu: Audit ki limitations hoti hain: cost, staff disruption, estimates uncertainty, concealed fraud, sampling, system limitations aur evidence ka persuasive hona. Isliye audit perfect guarantee nahi.
ch1_p23_s5.example: Management fake documents bana kar fraud hide kare to auditor ke liye detect karna mushkil ho sakta hai, especially jab collusion ho.

ch1_p23_s6.english: Because of these limitations, an audit provides reasonable assurance, not an absolute guarantee that financial statements are correct in every respect or that all fraud will be detected.
ch1_p23_s6.urdu: In limitations ki wajah se audit reasonable assurance deta hai, absolute assurance nahi. Auditor high level confidence deta hai, lekin yeh promise nahi karta ke har fraud ya error pakra gaya hai.
ch1_p23_s6.example: Clean audit report ke baad bhi future mein small error ya hidden fraud discover ho sakta hai, kyunke auditor sampling aur judgement use karta hai.
